content: The brain's prefrontal-hippocampal interaction during memory retrieval can serve as a biological blueprint for designing more efficient tool-use autonomous agents with function calling. Just as the medial prefrontal cortex coordinates hippocampal memory traces to retrieve context-relevant past associations to solve current problems, an agent's top-level 'prefrontal' controller can query a 'hippocampal' memory bank of past tool-use trajectories to select contextually appropriate function calls for new tasks.
source_topics: tool use function calling autonomous agent
